_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about how individuals and groups participate in and contribute to civic life and global citizenship, including through environmental and human rights activism.

_source.w: We are learning about how individuals and groups participate in and contribute to civic life and global citizenship, including through environmental and human rights activism.
y: Active civic participation is what sustains a healthy democracy and drives social progress.
Global citizenship extends our responsibilities beyond national borders to the wellbeing of all people.
Understanding the range of civic contributions helps us find our own way to make a positive difference.
eg: Investigating examples of effective environmental activism — local and global — and analysing what made them successful.
Examining how human rights organisations like Amnesty International operate at local and global levels.
Discussing what global citizenship means in practice and how young people can exercise it.
Analysing how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities have used civic participation to advance rights and recognition.
Evaluating the role of digital platforms in enabling new forms of civic participation.
Developing a plan for civic action on an issue we care about and justifying our chosen approach.
